goal:
You are a self-improving AI agent building your own capabilities.

=== YOUR COMPLETE CODEBASE ===

--- agent.py (737 lines) ---
  Classes: MLXAgent:
  Functions: __init__, _format_prompt, _generate_response, _extract_tool_calls, execute_tool, _web_search, _run_python, _bash, _read_file, _write_file
  Purpose: MLX-powered autonomous agent for Apple Silicon.

Uses Qwen3's NATIVE Hermes tool-calling format (<tool_call>) and
tokeni

--- config.py (54 lines) ---
  Classes: ModelConfig:, AgentConfig:
  Purpose: Agent configuration for M4 Max (36GB). Max context = max RAM usage.

--- failing_test.py (3 lines) ---
  Functions: test_add

--- improve.py (337 lines) ---
  Functions: load_history, save_history, scan_codebase, scan_history, build_goal, discover_output_file, validate_output, run_cycle, main
  Purpose: Self-aware improvement loop.

Each cycle the agent:
1. Reads the ENTIRE codebase
2. Reads what it has already built (age

--- logger.py (164 lines) ---
  Classes: AgentLogger:
  Functions: __init__, _write, run_start, step_start, generation, tool_call, tool_result, decision, phase_change, loop_detected
  Purpose: Structured logging for every agent decision, tool call, and result.

--- memory.py (96 lines) ---
  Classes: Iteration:, SessionMemory:, MemoryManager:
  Functions: add_iteration, save, load, __init__, record_attempt, record_discovery, record_failure, record_success
  Purpose: Agent session memory - tracks iterations, discoveries, and failures.

--- missing_import.py (5 lines) ---
  Functions: test_add

--- monitor.py (311 lines) ---
  Functions: freshness, get_agent_process, get_memory, get_perf, get_cycle_stats, get_output_files, get_log_tail, build_dashboard, main
  Purpose: Real-time agent performance monitor for M4 Max.

--- setup.py (200 lines) ---
  Functions: run, check_hardware, setup_venv, install_packages, install_brew_packages, download_models, create_directories, validate, print_usage, main
  Purpose: Setup script for MLX Agent on Apple Silicon.

Installs all dependencies, downloads models, validates environment.
Run on

--- skill_tree.py (501 lines) ---
  Classes: Skill:
  Functions: get_system_state, get_next_skill, get_full_codebase_summary, get_tree_as_text, build_goal_for_skill, load_proposals, print_tree
  Purpose: Skill Tree: A directed graph of agent capabilities.

Each skill has:
- Prerequisites (what must be built first)
- Priori

--- syntax_error.py (3 lines) ---
  Functions: test_add

--- valid.py (3 lines) ---
  Functions: test_add

--- agent_outputs/ ---
  code_validator.py (144 lines): import ast
  decision_maker.py (51 lines): import json
  error_recovery.py (73 lines): class ErrorRecovery:
  memory_compressor.py (58 lines): import json
  metrics.py (58 lines): class AgentMetrics:
  search_cache.py (63 lines): import time

=== YOUR SKILL TREE (what you're building toward) ===

TIER 1: FOUNDATION
  [DONE] Search Result Cache -> search_cache.py (impact:7/10)
  [DONE] Performance Metrics -> metrics.py (impact:6/10)
  [DONE] Memory Compression -> memory_compressor.py (impact:6/10)
  [DONE] Error Recovery -> error_recovery.py (impact:8/10)
  [DONE] Code Validator -> code_validator.py (impact:9/10)

TIER 2: INTELLIGENCE
  [READY] Loop Detector -> loop_detector.py (impact:8/10) (needs: metrics)
  [READY] Confidence Scorer -> confidence_scorer.py (impact:9/10) (needs: metrics, error_recovery)
  [READY] Result Evaluator -> result_evaluator.py (impact:7/10) (needs: search_cache)
  [READY] Task Planner -> task_planner.py (impact:8/10) (needs: error_recovery)

TIER 3: INTEGRATION
  [LOCKED] Smart Tool Router -> smart_router.py (impact:9/10) (needs: confidence_scorer, loop_detector, task_planner)
  [LOCKED] Self Evaluator -> self_evaluator.py (impact:10/10) (needs: code_validator, result_evaluator, metrics)

TIER 4: OPTIMIZATION
  [LOCKED] Agent Orchestrator -> orchestrator.py (impact:10/10) (needs: smart_router, self_evaluator)
  [LOCKED] Strategy Learner -> strategy_learner.py (impact:10/10) (needs: orchestrator, self_evaluator)

=== YOUR CURRENT TASK ===
BUILD: Confidence Scorer (Tier 2: Intelligence, Impact: 9/10)
FILE: confidence_scorer.py
WHY: Score agent confidence: should it act, research more, or ask for help?

SPEC:
Class ConfidenceScorer with: score_knowledge(discoveries_count, relevant_keywords)->float 0-1, score_capability(tool_success_rate, errors_count)->float 0-1, score_progress(steps_taken, max_steps, files_written)->float 0-1, should_act(knowledge, capability, progress)->str returning 'research','code','save','abort', overall(knowledge, capability, progress)->float 0-1 combined score.

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
Test: low knowledge=research, high everything=save, many errors=abort, balanced=code

=== EXPANDING THE SYSTEM ===
After building this skill, if you see an opportunity to improve the skill tree
itself (add new skills, refine specs, identify missing capabilities), you can
write suggestions to agent_outputs/tree_proposals.txt using write_file.
Each proposal should have: skill name, file, tier, prereqs, description, spec.

=== RULES ===
1. Read ALL source files you need to understand the full system
2. Read existing agent_outputs/*.py to understand what's built
3. Write COMPLETE working code in confidence_scorer.py
4. Include 'if __name__ == "__main__":' test block
5. Tests MUST print 'ALL TESTS PASSED' when everything works
6. Don't import agent.py in tests (loads MLX model, too slow)
7. CAN import from memory.py, config.py, or other agent_outputs/*.py
8. Fix errors until tests pass, then say DONE
